note: Backend (localhost:8080) is not running. Audit based on static code analysis of 149 controllers, 265 entities, and security configuration. See findings for detailed contract violations and architectural issues.

findings.description: List endpoint requires permissions: EMPLOYEE_VIEW_ALL OR EMPLOYEE_VIEW_DEPARTMENT OR EMPLOYEE_VIEW_TEAM. Implements pagination (page, size, sortBy, sortDirection). Multiple permission support means tenant-aware filtering.
Search endpoint with query parameter. Handles null/empty query gracefully (BUG-002 FIX: returns all employees instead of 500 error). Requires same permissions as GET /employees.
Public login endpoint. Accepts LoginRequest with email and password. Sets secure httpOnly cookies. Valid input is enforced via @Valid decorator. Rate limiting: 5 requests/min per IP.
Public MFA second-factor endpoint. Requires userId and MFA code. Returns 401 if code is invalid. Supports TOTP codes (6 digits) and backup codes (longer). Properly secured despite being public.
Public tenant registration endpoint. Permits public access per SecurityConfig line 120. Creates new tenant and initial admin user.
Public offer portal endpoint (token-based access). No authentication required. Token validates candidate and offer association. Per SecurityConfig line 131.
Public career portal endpoint. Lists active job postings. No authentication required. Per SecurityConfig line 135.
Public webhook endpoint for payment providers (Razorpay, Stripe, etc). No authentication required. Signature verification is delegated to provider-specific handlers. Per SecurityConfig line 141.
Public health check endpoint. Returns 200 with component status. No authentication required per SecurityConfig line 122.
Public detailed health check endpoints (db, redis, kafka, elasticsearch, minio, etc). No authentication required per SecurityConfig line 122.

All other actuator endpoints (metrics, env, beans, mappings) require SUPER_ADMIN role per SecurityConfig line 123. Risk: if JWT is compromised or role escalation exists, actuator exposes sensitive system info.
Swagger UI requires SUPER_ADMIN role per SecurityConfig line 126. In dev profile, SwaggerSecurityConfig may override this. Risk: API documentation exposes endpoint structure and parameter names.
Create employee endpoint requires EMPLOYEE_CREATE permission. Validates input via @Valid CreateEmployeeRequest. Returns 201 on success, 400 on invalid input, 409 if code/email exists.
All auth endpoints are public per SecurityConfig line 115. CSRF is ignored for auth endpoints (line 172) because they are public and don't require existing auth. Proper design.
Permissions list endpoint. Likely returns all 500+ permissions in the system. MEMORY.md references 04_RBAC_PERMISSION_MATRIX.md with 500+ permissions. Requires permission check before returning.
Departments list endpoint. Likely paginated. Requires permission check before returning. Tenant-aware filtering applied.
Attendance records list endpoint. Likely paginated. Requires permission check. Tenant-aware and potentially employee-aware filtering.
Leave requests list endpoint. Paginated. Requires permission check. Includes approval workflow state.

Payroll records endpoint. CRITICAL: Payroll data is sensitive and highly regulated. Requires strong permission checks. Should filter by employee scope (self, team, department, all). Risk: overly permissive access exposes salary data.
Recruitment pipeline endpoint. Lists job postings, candidates, offers. Requires RECRUITMENT_VIEW or similar permission. Workflow state tracked.

Performance reviews endpoint. Sensitive data (feedback, ratings, development plans). Requires permission checks. Should filter by: self-reviews, reviews-of-direct-reports, assigned-reviewers, admin-access.
Training & LMS endpoint. Lists courses, enrollments, progress. Requires permission checks. Employee self-service includes assigned trainings.
Document management endpoint. Sensitive: employee contracts, offer letters, compliance docs. Requires permission checks. Should filter by: created-by, assigned-to, departmental-scope.
Notifications list endpoint. Each user sees only their own notifications due to tenant isolation and user filtering. Properly scoped.
Users list endpoint. Requires permission check. Should filter by tenant scope. Risk: exposes user emails, statuses, roles.
Asset management endpoint. Lists company assets (laptops, phones, furniture, etc). Requires permission check. Tenant-aware filtering.
WebSocket endpoints for real-time notifications. Per SecurityConfig line 137, permits public access. Authentication handled at STOMP protocol level via JWT in connection headers.
Leave balance endpoint. Employee can view own balance (self-service). Managers can view team balances. Admins can view all. Proper permission layering required.
Roles list endpoint. Exposes role definitions and hierarchy. Should require admin permission. Risk: reveals organizational structure and access levels.
Workflow definitions and instances. Lists approval workflows, their steps, and current state. Requires permission check. Tenant-aware.
Organization structure endpoint. Lists org hierarchy, departments, locations. Tenant-aware. Self-service employees should see their own org context.

122 out of 149 controllers use @Valid for request body validation. 27 controllers lack @Valid annotations. Potential for 400-level errors if validation is skipped. Recommend audit of remaining 27 controllers.
Self-service endpoint returning authenticated user's own employee profile. Avoids need to know own UUID. Proper implementation per CLAUDE.md architectural notes.

Rate limiting enabled via RateLimitingFilter. Auth endpoints: 5 req/min. API endpoints: 100 req/min. Export endpoints: 5 req/5min. Uses Redis-backed distributed rate limiting with in-memory fallback. Per application-dev.yml.

CORS configured per SecurityConfig.java corsConfigurationSource(). Allowed origins: http://localhost:3000, http://localhost:3001, http://localhost:8080. Credentials allowed. No wildcard origins (P1.2 fix).

CSRF protection via double-submit cookie pattern. Enabled by default unless app.security.csrf.enabled=false in non-prod profiles. Token stored in httpOnly cookie, sent back in X-XSRF-TOKEN header. Per SecurityConfig line 157-187.

All responses include OWASP security headers: Strict-Transport-Security (1 year), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Content-Security-Policy, Permissions-Policy. Per SecurityConfig line 85-109.

JWT-based stateless authentication. JWT valid for 1 hour (3600000ms). Refresh token valid for 24 hours. Roles stored in JWT; permissions loaded from DB via SecurityService.getCachedPermissions(). Per CLAUDE.md RBAC design.

Tenant isolation enforced via TenantFilter (added before UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter per SecurityConfig line 146). All queries must include tenant_id WHERE clause. PostgreSQL RLS policies enforce DB-level isolation. Per CLAUDE.md multi-tenancy design.
DocuSign webhook endpoint. Public per SecurityConfig line 139. HMAC signature verification applied per CRIT-002 security requirement. CSRF ignored for this endpoint (line 185).

Preboarding portal endpoints for new hires. Token-based access. No authentication required per SecurityConfig line 143. New hires complete pre-boarding tasks (tax forms, direct deposit, etc.) before first day.
Public exit interview endpoint. Token-based access for departing employees. No authentication required per SecurityConfig line 133. CSRF ignored (line 176).
External e-signature endpoint. Token-based access. No authentication required per SecurityConfig line 129. Candidates sign offer letters, contracts externally.

Create leave request endpoint. Triggers approval workflow. Requires LEAVE_REQUEST_CREATE permission. Validation: dates, leave balance, overlapping requests. Approval state machine managed by WorkflowEngine.

Create payroll run endpoint. Requires PAYROLL_CREATE permission. Risk: calculations are formula-based (SpEL) evaluated in dependency order (DAG). All calculations wrapped in DB transaction. Per CLAUDE.md payroll design.
Benefits list endpoint. Employee self-service shows enrolled benefits. Admins see all benefit definitions and enrollments. Requires permission check. Tenant-aware.
Compliance module endpoint. Tracks regulations, audits, certifications. Requires admin permission. Sensitive: exposes company's compliance posture.
Analytics dashboard endpoint. Aggregated metrics: headcount, attrition, salary trends, etc. Requires ANALYTICS_VIEW permission. Tenant-aware filtering.
Audit log endpoint. Lists all recorded actions (creates, updates, deletes, approvals). Requires AUDIT_VIEW permission. Essential for compliance. Immutable records.
Surveys & pulse endpoint. Employee engagement surveys. Requires permission. Response anonymity may vary by survey type. Tenant-aware.
Applicant tracking endpoint. Lists candidates, pipeline state, interview feedback. Requires RECRUITMENT_VIEW permission. Filters by job, stage, and user role.
Contract management endpoint. Employment contracts, templates, e-signature tracking. Requires permission. Sensitive: legal agreements. Tenant-aware.
Expense claims endpoint. Employee submits receipts for reimbursement. Requires approval workflow. Managers approve. Finance reconciles. Workflow-driven.
Tax declaration endpoint. Section 80C, HRA, professional tax forms. Requires permission. Sensitive: personal tax info. Tenant-aware. Employee self-service includes personal declarations.
File upload endpoint. Used across modules (documents, contracts, expense receipts, etc). Requires permission check. File size limits, type validation recommended. Stored in MinIO (S3-compatible).
Data export endpoint. Employees, payroll, attendance, etc. Rate limited: 5 req/5min per IP. Requires permission. Exports to CSV, Excel. Sensitive data filtering based on user role.
Dashboard endpoint. Personalized home page for each user. Shows relevant cards/widgets based on role and data scope. Employee sees self-service cards, managers see team data.

Knowledge management search endpoint (NU-Fluence module). Uses Elasticsearch. Searches wikis, blogs, templates. Requires permission. Tenant-aware. Phase 2: backend built, frontend UI not started per CLAUDE.md.
Meeting scheduler endpoint. One-on-ones, team meetings, interviews. Integration with Google Calendar. Requires permission. Calendar sync.
Employee loans endpoint. Salary advances, education loans. Requires approval workflow. Finance reconciliation. Deductions from payroll.
Employee referral program endpoint. Track referred candidates, payouts, status. Incentives management.
Overtime tracking endpoint. Records OT hours, approval, compensation. Integration with attendance and payroll.
Mobile API endpoints (attendance, leave, approvals, notifications, sync). Separate controllers optimized for mobile. Potentially different permission models.
Error endpoint. Public per SecurityConfig line 114. Spring automatically routes unhandled exceptions here. Should return generic error messages in production (not stack traces).

critical_findings: Backend is not running on localhost:8080. Cannot perform live API contract tests.
27 out of 149 controllers lack @Valid annotations for request body validation.
Actuator endpoints require SUPER_ADMIN role — security-sensitive system info exposed if JWT compromised.
Swagger UI requires SUPER_ADMIN role — API structure exposed if role escalation vulnerability exists.
Payroll and tax endpoints are highly sensitive — require audit of permission enforcement.
Performance review endpoint requires careful permission scoping — prevent unauthorized access to sensitive feedback.
Contract and document endpoints store legal agreements — require strong access controls.
high_priority_fixes: Start backend: docker-compose up -d && cd backend && ./start-backend.sh && cd frontend && npm run dev
Audit permission checks for: payroll, performance reviews, tax declarations, contracts, compliance
Verify CORS headers are correctly set (no wildcard origins)
Test rate limiting with rapid requests to /api/v1/auth/login (expect 429 after 5/min)
Verify tenant isolation enforcement at database level via PostgreSQL RLS
Test CSRF token generation and validation
Verify all webhook endpoints have proper signature verification (DocuSign HMAC, Payment provider signatures)
security_architecture_notes: JWT tokens contain roles only (not permissions) to keep cookies < 4096 bytes per CRIT-001.
Permissions loaded from DB via SecurityService.getCachedPermissions() in JwtAuthenticationFilter.
SuperAdmin role automatically bypasses ALL access control (RequiresPermission, RequiresFeature).
Tenant isolation enforced at TenantFilter layer and PostgreSQL RLS policies.
Rate limiting: 5/min auth, 100/min API, 5/5min exports. Uses Redis-backed distributed rate limiting with in-memory fallback.
Public endpoints: auth, tenant registration, public careers/offers, payment/docusign webhooks, preboarding, exit interview, e-signature externals.
CSRF protection: double-submit cookie pattern. Ignored for public endpoints and webhooks.
CORS: configured for localhost:3000, localhost:3001, localhost:8080. No wildcards.
Security headers: HSTS (1 year), CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy.
architectural_observations: All 254 DB tables include tenant_id column for multi-tenancy.
265 entities, 260 repositories, 454 DTOs — well-structured domain model.
Forms use React Hook Form + Zod on frontend, @Valid on backend.
Approval workflows: data-driven via WorkflowEngine, not hardcoded.
Payroll: formula-based using Spring Expression Language (SpEL), DAG evaluation, wrapped in transaction.
Leave accrual: scheduled Cron job (Quartz), monthly accrual, deduction on approval.
Kafka topics: nu-aura.approvals, nu-aura.notifications, nu-aura.audit, nu-aura.employee-lifecycle, nu-aura.fluence-content (5 topics + 5 DLT topics).
WebSocket: STOMP + SockJS for real-time notifications.
Mobile APIs: separate optimized endpoints for mobile clients.
